taxonomy: Thonandia longifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Thonandia, which is a part of the larger order Sapindales. The family Sapindaceae is a group of plants commonly known as soapberry or soapbush family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphological features. Thonandia longifolia's classification is closely related to other plants in the same family, which are often found in tropical and subtropical regions.
uses: Thonandia longifolia is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its roots are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. Thonandia longifolia is also used to treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties are thought to contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Thonandia longifolia.

preparations: Thonandia longifolia has been traditionally used in herbal preparations, particularly in Southeast Asian folk medicine, to treat various ailments such as fever and digestive issues.
